title: Ischnosiphon arouma
taxonomy: Ischnosiphon arouma is classified within the family Stemonuraceae. This family is part of the order Celastrales, which is a group of flowering plants. Ischnosiphon arouma further belongs to the genus Ischnosiphon, a group of woody plants native to Central and South America. The classification of Ischnosiphon arouma is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. Further research may be required to refine its taxonomic classification.

intro: Ischnosiphon arouma, also known as Tafalla, is a type of plant belonging to the Stemonuraceae family, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is rich in medicinal properties and has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ischnosiphon arouma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Ischnosiphon arouma is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
